records.ety: [L. difficultas, fr. difficilis difficult; dif- = dis- + facilis easy: cf. F. difficult. See Facile.]

defs: The state of being difficult, or hard to do; hardness; arduousness; -- opposed to easiness or facility; as, the difficulty of a task or enterprise; a work of difficulty.
quotes: Not being able to promote them [the interests of life] on account of the difficulty of the region.

defs: Something difficult; a thing hard to do or to understand; that which occasions labor or perplexity, and requires skill and perseverance to overcome, solve, or achieve; a hard enterprise; an obstacle; an impediment; as, the difficulties of a science; difficulties in theology.
quotes: They lie under some difficulties by reason of the emperor's displeasure.

defs: A controversy; a falling out; a disagreement; an objection; a cavil.
quotes: Measures for terminating all local difficulties.

defs: Embarrassment of affairs, especially financial affairs; -- usually in the plural; as, to be in difficulties.
quotes: In days of difficulty and pressure.
